execution_overview.overall_summary: Built a Python Docker environment, updated the entrypoint to a new Python script using the original CSVs, and executed both planned tasks. Results indicate High openness has lower ASPL and lower modularity Q than Low (supporting greater interconnectivity and lower modularity), while CC for High is slightly lower (not higher) than Low. Both tasks completed successfully.

deviations.description: Replaced corrupted original Python script with a new Python entry script that merges FINAL fluency.csv with FINAL open.csv and implements the planned semantic network and partial bootstrap analyses.
deviations.reason: Original code referenced a non-existent .sav file and contained syntax/logic corruption, preventing execution.

deviations.description: Added lightweight runtime pip installation in entry.py for numpy/pandas/scikit-learn/networkx/python-louvain if missing.
deviations.reason: Container execution initially failed due to ModuleNotFoundError: numpy.

deviations.description: Switched merge source from a missing .sav to the available FINAL open.csv for openness scores.
deviations.reason: The .sav file referenced in the old code was not present; FINAL open.csv contained suitable openness measures (LATENT/AVERAGE/no_int).

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned path: used original verbal fluency and openness data, split by openness, built cosine networks, computed ASPL/CC/Q, and ran partial node-retention bootstraps (50–90%).

result_raw.result_text: At 90% node retention, High openness had lower ASPL than Low (mean diff = -0.0255; t-like ≈ -33.51), lower CC (mean diff ≈ -0.0066), and lower modularity Q (mean diff ≈ -0.0363).

conclusion.conclusion_text: Task1 supports the focal claim that higher openness relates to more interconnected semantic networks (lower ASPL) and lower modularity (lower Q); clustering coefficient (CC) was lower for High, not higher, indicating partial but substantial support.

deviations.description: Used new Python entry script and updated analysis_info.json to point to data/entry.py.
deviations.reason: Original code was incompatible/corrupted and referenced missing .sav.

deviations.description: Added runtime dependency bootstrap to entry.py.
deviations.reason: Resolved missing numpy error during container execution.

deviations.description: Merged with FINAL open.csv instead of missing .sav.
deviations.reason: Ensure openness measure is available for group split.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the planned 90% node-retention bootstrap exactly using the same preprocessing, graph construction, and metrics.

result_raw.result_text: With 90% nodes retained, High openness showed lower ASPL than Low (mean diff = -0.0255; t-like ≈ -33.51), lower CC (≈ -0.0066), and lower Q (≈ -0.0364).

conclusion.conclusion_text: Task2 supports the focal claim on interconnectivity and modularity (ASPL and Q lower for High), though CC was lower for High rather than higher.
